tasks.description: resolveImplementerBackend resolver in lib/routing.mjs — the dispatch-descriptor sibling of routeTask. Tagged union: {kind:'agent'} reproduces shipping; {kind:'qctl'} only when config.implementer.qctl.enabled === true.
Attach the backend descriptor in prepareWave (lib/wave.mjs) — leanPayload gains a backend param+key; prepareWave calls resolveImplementerBackend per task.

Resolve the descriptor in the follow prose (commands/masterplan.md) — follow verb step 3 resolves resolveImplementerBackend instead of hard-coding mp-implementer; qctl kind surfaces NotYetBound, never silently falls back.
Document the flag (docs/config-schema.md) — insert the default-off implementer.qctl.enabled block.
Thread state.implementer into the prepare-wave config (bin/masterplan.mjs) — the prepare-wave case threads implementer: state.implementer ?? {} into the config passed to prepareWave.
NotYetBound guard in implement(t) (workflows/execute.workflow.js) — a qctl kind returns a blocked digest (qctl-not-bound); the agent path stays byte-for-byte identical.
Full-suite green + byte-identical audit (flag off ⇒ dispatch path unchanged) + Codex defect pass before declaring done. Nothing pushed without explicit user approval.
